title: THE BLACK EARLY LEARNING PROGRAM - 211info
text: EARLY CHILDHOOD HUB OF LANE COUNTY THE BLACK EARLY LEARNING PROGRAM Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides developmental screening, referrals to educational and community resources, learning opportunities, connects children with other Black children, and supports parents as they help children learn and grow. 3171 Gateway Loop Springfield, OR 97477 Eligibility: Ages 0-6 African American/Black children Hours: Varies Email (541) 357-5719 Get Directions Visit Website Phone/FAX Numbers Intake (541) 357-5719 Early Learning Coordinator Ayanna Moriguchi Email Address: [email protected] INTAKE PROCEDURE: Visit website to fill out inquiry form or call for more information FEES: None DOCUMENTS: None required at intake AREA SERVED: Lane County Services: Child Care Provider Referrals * African American Community Children's Play Groups * African American Community Family Support Centers/Outreach * African American Community Pediatric Developmental Screening * African American Community Specialized Information and Referral * African American Community Other Services Offered At This Location: INDIVIDUAL AND FAMILY SUPPORT SERVICES - FAMILY RESOURCE CENTERS PUBLIC ASSISTANCE PROGRAMS - PRESCHOOL PROMISE If you represent this agency, update your listing here . * The community resource directory information is up to date to the best of our knowledge. However, you should always call the provider to confirm this information and make an appointment. Be sure to confirm payment information with the provider, if payment is required. 211Info does not rate, recommend or endorse any agency. We simply provide information as a public service. EARLY CHILDHOOD HUB OF LANE COUNTY
